fields.label: Attendance Mode
fields.description: How the mobile app records attendance for payroll.

fields.options:
Checkin Based — the big Check In / Check Out button captures GPS + selfie and writes an Employee Checkin row. Task timers (Tasks tab) only write Timesheet entries — they do not create checkin rows, so attendance is never double-counted.

Timer Based — the Check In button first asks which task the user is starting. Starting a task writes Employee Checkin (IN) + opens a Timesheet row. Stopping the task writes Employee Checkin (OUT) + closes the row. The Tasks tab and Check-in button share one live timer.

Payroll reads Employee Checkin rows → auto-Attendance → payment_days on Salary Slip. Choose the mode that matches how your team works.

In Timer Based mode, every employee must have at least one open Task assigned (status = Open / Working, _assign contains the user). If not, the Check-in button will show an error asking them to contact HR.
